title: cneorum pulverulentum
intro: Cneorum pulverulentum, also known as the dusty miller or white rockrose, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cneorum pulverulentum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and act as an antioxidant, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin care and wound healing. Cneorum pulverulentum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical cream, making it a versatile herb for various applications.
